one_liner: WEN +7.84% continuing the 'Wendependence Day' meme squeeze recovery — the June 24 Selling Climax (11.54x volume) set the floor and the stock is grinding up from post-climax lows.

price_action.vol_rank: Above average but well below climax levels
price_action.intraday_story: WEN opened at $8.30, touched $8.20 briefly, then rallied to $8.98 near the close — almost a clean inside day relative to last week's swings, with the stock grinding toward the $9 psychological level. The 1.25x volume is solidly above average but a fraction of the 11.54x Selling Climax on June 24 and 5.21x the next day. Volume is normalizing, which is what you'd expect after a climactic event.

news_catalyst.headline: Wendy's 'Wendependence Day' meme campaign, new CFO Steve Cirulis, and Russell 2000 Value index inclusion driving short squeeze continuation
news_catalyst.summary:
Wendy's has been caught in a perfect storm of catalysts since June 24. The original Selling Climax (11.54x volume on June 24) coincided with a viral 'Save Wendy's' campaign on r/WallStreetBets and the announcement that Steve Cirulis was appointed CFO — the same executive who helped CEO Bob Wright engineer a successful turnaround at Potbelly. This executive reunion is seen as the strongest long-term fundamental signal in the recent rally.

The 26.4% short interest created enormous short squeeze pressure: at its peak (June 24), trading was halted multiple times as the stock surged 40% from the previous close. The Russell 2000 Value-Defensive Index addition (effective June 29) added passive fund buying on top of the squeeze dynamics. By today (July 1), the 'Wendependence Day' narrative has settled into more normal grinding price action with declining but above-average volume — the acute squeeze phase has passed but the new CFO/turnaround narrative provides a fundamental floor.

technical.summary:
The Wyckoff picture here is textbook Selling Climax (SC) → Automatic Rally (AR) → Secondary Test (ST) sequence. The June 24 session (11.54x volume, tagged as 'Selling climax') established the panic low. June 25 (5.21x, Selling Climax) was a second flush. June 26 (3.72x, Buying Climax interpretation) confirmed the AR as buyers stepped in aggressively.

Since the SC/AR, volume has been declining in orderly steps: 3.72x → 1.64x → 1.30x → 1.25x — classic volume compression in the post-climax accumulation range. The near-term support is $7.22 (June 25 intraday low) and resistance is $9.07 (June 25 intraday high). Today's close near $8.94 is approaching that resistance level — a clean break above $9.07 on volume 1.5x+ would signal Phase C initiation and a potential markup leg.

wyckoff_narrative:
WEN's recent price action is one of the cleaner Wyckoff patterns in the watchlist despite the meme overlay. The June 24 Selling Climax (11.54x volume) represents the type of panic low that exhausts sellers — when 10x+ normal volume hits a stock at multi-year lows and the price reverses, the supply is definitively cleared. The subsequent Buying Climax on June 26 (3.72x) and declining volume over June 29-July 1 confirms absorption.

The meme catalyst noise doesn't change the Wyckoff interpretation — the SC/BC pattern exists regardless of what caused it. The new CFO announcement is the fundamental anchor that distinguishes this from a pure meme play. Next expected event: a Secondary Test (ST) of the June 24-25 low zone ($7.22-7.33) on low volume, which would confirm the SC as the price floor. If that test holds, the stock enters Phase C with a potential markup to the $10+ area.

valuation.summary: DCF data not available. Wendy's trades at a low P/E with a high dividend yield — the fundamental case for value has been noted (low P/E + huge yield attracted WSB 'save Wendy's' narrative). However, the restaurant sector faces margin pressure from labor costs and weak consumer spending. The new CFO is the real value catalyst — track execution.

key_strengths: New CFO Steve Cirulis: prior turnaround success at Potbelly alongside current CEO
Russell 2000 Value index addition creates passive buying floor
Low P/E and high dividend yield provide value screen support
key_concerns: 26.4% short interest — squeeze dynamics can reverse rapidly
Restaurant fundamentals: labor costs, consumer wallet pressure
Prior price was $6.26 before the squeeze — beware pricing in the turnaround too early
fundamentals.summary: The Cirulis CFO appointment is the only fundamental change here. The squeeze dynamics created a higher price platform, but the real question is whether Cirulis can actually execute the turnaround. Track Q2 results carefully.

risks: Short squeeze has significantly compressed — 26.4% short interest was the fuel; as the squeeze resolves, that tailwind disappears
Meme stocks fade: if WSB attention moves to another ticker, WEN could give back half the gains within a week
A pullback to test the $7.22-7.33 SC low is part of the normal Wyckoff sequence — don't chase; wait for the test
